sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The changes are at rows 61-62, cols 39-41. These are the HUD row and the row below it. The red dot at (61, 40) and some N-colored cells disappeared.
So the red dot at row 61 was part of the HUD, not the main game area. It disappeared after growing green. This suggests that the red dot indicates which item needs to be adjusted, and when that item is "done", the red dot disappears.
But wait, N didn't change size. The items are all the same. So maybe the red dot indicates the item that needs to be adjusted, and growing green "completed" the green item somehow.
Let me think about this differently. The red dot at row 61 was at col 40, which is in the green container's column range (cols 39-41). Maybe the red dot indicates which container's item needs adjustment. When I grow green, the green item is "completed", and the red dot disappears.
But the level is not complete yet. So there must be more items to complete.
Let me check if there are any other red dots or indicators. The cross pattern at (31, 52) is still there. Maybe that's the target for the next item.
Actually, looking at the image, the red cross is near the L-shape's right vertical bar. Maybe it indicates the target position for the green item (N). N is at cols 39-41, and the cross is at col 52. But N is a vertical bar, so its horizontal position doesn't match the cross's horizontal position.
Wait, let me reconsider. The red dot at row 61 was at col 40, which is the center of N's column range (39-41). When I grew green, the red dot disappeared. This suggests that the green item was "selected" or "active", and growing it completed the green item.
Now, with no red dot at row 61, maybe the level is waiting for me to select another item. Or maybe the level is complete and I need to confirm.
Actually, the level is not complete (`level_completed: false`). So there's more to do.
Let me check the current state more carefully. Maybe there's a new red dot or indicator that appeared.
Actually, looking at the segmentation, there are 4 red dots (the cross pattern). These are at (30, 52), (31, 51), (31, 53), (32, 52). This cross is near the L-shape's right vertical bar (cols 42-44).
Maybe the cross indicates the target position for the next item. The items are:
- Y: rows 27-29, cols 28-38
- S: rows 30-40, cols 27-29
- O: rows 39-41, cols 10-26
- N: rows 28-53, cols 39-41
The cross is at (31, 52), which is to the right of all items. Maybe I need to move an item to that position.
But I don't have a way to move items horizontally except by growing/shrinking orange, which moves the entire assembly left/right.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the game mechanics. Maybe each container controls a different aspect of the puzzle:
- Orange container: moves the assembly left/right
- Blue container: moves the assembly up/down (or does nothing)
- Yellow container: adjusts Y's size
- Green container: adjusts N's size
If the red dot at row 61 indicated the "active" item, and it disappeared after completing green, then maybe I need to activate another item.
But how do I activate an item? Maybe by clicking on its container's U-shapes.
Let me try clicking on the yellow container's right U-shape (col 39) to see if it activates yellow.
Actually, I already tried that earlier, and it was no-impact (yellow at min). But maybe the semantics have changed now that green is "completed".
Let me try growing yellow again.
